title: Quick Start
body: Tokenomics is an OpenAI-compatible reverse proxy with wrapper tokens and policy controls.
- Create wrapper token: tokenomics token create --policy '{...}'
- Run a command with ephemeral proxy: tokenomics run <command>
- run reuses an already healthy local proxy when available
- run only enables admin when --admin is set and admin.enabled=true

title: Configuration Guide
body: Resolution priority: CLI flags, environment variables, config file, then defaults.
- .tokenomics path is relative to current working directory unless overridden
- use --dir or config dir to pin a stable location

title: Policy Authoring
body: Policy has global fields plus provider-specific policy arrays.
- provider base_key_env, upstream_url, max_tokens, model, model_regex, timeout replace global values
- provider prompts prepend before global prompts
- provider rules append after global rules
- policy must include global base_key_env or at least one provider policy
- invalid regex patterns fail policy parse
- memory is treated as tool-level configuration in this admin workflow, not token policy input

title: Policy Editor Usage
body: The key form is modal based.
- New Key opens policy editor modal
- View loads an existing key policy
- Save updates or creates, then closes the editor on success
- Delete removes key and closes the editor on success
- role options: system, developer, user, assistant, custom
- Model regex field is pattern match
- Test regex button tests the current model regex against user input
- each row maps one provider name to one provider policy JSON object
- you can add multiple rows for the same provider
- Save always posts policy as JSON string
- after create, raw key appears in one-time modal with copy button

title: Admin API Reference
body: - returns raw token once, hash, and expires
- returns current process environment variable names

title: Admin Security Model
body: Admin routes are local-only by middleware.
Requests from non-loopback clients are rejected.
Optional basic auth applies to all admin UI and API routes when configured.
- keep admin.enabled=true only for local operator environments
- set admin.auth credentials when remote access could occur
- keep TLS enabled for persistent serve where possible

title: Process Lifecycle and Reliability
body: Only start, stop, and run manage background process behavior.
- resolves health endpoint from host, port, and tls flags
- checks healthy endpoint first and reports already running
- reconciles stale PID file where possible
- retries startup for transient DB lock contention
- cleans up spawned daemon process on readiness failure
- terminates by PID and process group
- falls back to process scan for stale or missing PID state
- removes PID file after successful shutdown
- reuses healthy local proxy when present
- starts ephemeral serve only when needed
- cleans up ephemeral process group on exit or signal
doctor can flag macOS binary execution policy risk for /var/tmp binaries.
